prerequisites: An AWS account, with its account identifier available for the optional aws_account_id field.
An existing Amazon S3 bucket where the connector writes data; its name goes in the required bucket_name field (and an optional bucket_prefix to prepend to written objects).
An AWS Lake Formation database for table creation, named in the required lakeformation_database_name field.
The AWS region where these resources live, set in the required region field.

AWS credentials (an IAM user access key, or an IAM role) with access to AWS Lake Formation, AWS Glue, and Amazon S3, including the ability to write objects to the S3 bucket and update the Lake Formation database.

authMethods.name: IAM User access key
authMethods.summary: Authenticate as an IAM user by selecting the USER authentication mode and supplying an AWS Access Key ID and AWS Secret Access Key. These map to the connector secret fields credentials.aws_access_key_id and credentials.aws_secret_access_key. The IAM user must have permissions for AWS Lake Formation, AWS Glue, and Amazon S3.

setupSteps.title: Provision the AWS resources
setupSteps.body: Create or identify the Amazon S3 bucket for storage and the AWS Lake Formation database used for table creation, all in the same AWS region. Note the bucket name, database name, and region for the connector configuration.

setupSteps.title: Create an IAM user with the required access
setupSteps.body: Create or choose an IAM user that has access to AWS Lake Formation, AWS Glue, and Amazon S3. It must be able to write objects to the target S3 bucket and update the Lake Formation database. Generate an access key (Access Key ID and Secret Access Key) for this user.

setupSteps.title: Configure authentication
setupSteps.body: In the destination credentials, choose the USER authentication mode and enter the Access Key ID and Secret Access Key. These populate the secret fields credentials.aws_access_key_id and credentials.aws_secret_access_key.

setupSteps.title: Fill in the destination configuration
setupSteps.body: Set the required fields region, bucket_name, lakeformation_database_name, and credentials. Optionally set aws_account_id, bucket_prefix, format, partitioning, glue_catalog_float_as_decimal, lakeformation_governed_tables, and the lakeformation_database_default_tag_key / lakeformation_database_default_tag_values for database tagging.

setupSteps.title: Test the connection
setupSteps.body: Save and run the connection test so the connector can verify it can reach the bucket, the Glue and Lake Formation catalog, and write data.
